data.title: KeyBanc Remains Cautious on Apple Stock (AAPL) Ahead of Q1 Earnings
data.description:
Tech giant Apple (AAPL) is scheduled to announce its results for the first quarter of fiscal 2026 on January 29. Ahead of Q1 FY26 earnings, KeyBanc analyst Brandon Nispel reiterated a Hold rating on Apple stock, saying that he continues to have certain concerns about the iPhone maker. Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Apple to report earnings per share (EPS) of $2.67 for Q1 FY26, reflecting an 11.3% year-over-year growth. Revenue is also estimated to rise 11.3% to $138.38 billion.

data.title: IPO Alert: Ethos, Yellowstone, and PicPay Eye U.S. Debuts This Week (January 26–January 30)
data.description:
These are the upcoming IPOs (initial public offerings) scheduled for January 29, based on the TipRanks IPO Calendar. An IPO marks the first time a private company offers its shares to the public. After listing, the company's stock becomes available for trading on a public exchange.

Going public allows companies to raise new capital, increase visibility, and give early investors a chance to exit. At the same time, newly listed firms face tighter reporting rules and closer scrutiny from investors.

data.title: Upcoming Stock Splits This Week (January 26 to January 30) – Stay Invested
data.description:
These are the upcoming stock splits for the week of January 26 to January 30, based on TipRanks' Stock Splits Calendar. A stock split is one of the most common ways companies make their shares feel more accessible to everyday investors. The business doesn't suddenly become more valuable – its market cap stays exactly the same – but the stock is divided into more pieces, lowering the price per share and often attracting a broader pool of retail investors.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That's the familiar move. But some troubled companies take the opposite approach. A reverse stock split consolidates existing shares, shrinking the share count and pushing the price higher. Again, the valuation doesn't change, just the math. These moves are usually about survival rather than spectacle, helping companies meet exchange rules, especially minimum price thresholds on markets like Nasdaq, and avoid the risk of delisting.

Whether the goal is to welcome new investors or to stay listed at all, stock splits and reverse splits can offer useful signals about management's priorities – and about where the company believes its story is headed.

Let's take a look at the upcoming stock splits for the week.
data.author: Ben Yoffe

data.title: 'High Risk, High Reward,' Says Investor About Tesla Stock
data.description:
Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) certainly confounds any standard metrics for valuation. After all, TSLA's price-to-earnings ratio of roughly 300x is both significantly higher than the rest of the automotive sector and indicative of large amounts of future growth.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It seems pretty clear that the big bucks investors are hoping for are not coming from the company's current slate of EV offerings, as sales have slipped during the past two years. The company's 2025 EV deliveries were down about 9% from its 2024 numbers.

Instead, the hope is that Elon Musk and company will succeed in launching Tesla to the head of the autonomous driving class with its full self-driving technology. It goes without saying that Tesla bears are less optimistic that the company's performance will match its ambitions.

Investor Lee Samaha understands the doubters, especially given Musk's previous statements regarding robotaxi progress, which didn't quite come to fruition. Still, the 5-star investor thinks the company could be on the cusp of something big.

"The company's future hinges on its robotaxi rollout, and this is a critical year for the future of autonomous taxis," explains Samaha.

The investor argues that Tesla is not an "ordinary company" looking to make incremental earnings progress quarter after quarter. Instead, the company aims to make structural changes to the transportation industry as a whole.

In that sense, Samaha posits, worries about hitting its stated objectives exactly on the nose miss the forest for the trees.

"Tesla leads the race to revolutionize how consumers think about mobility," adds the investor. "If milestones are reached a few quarters later than expected, it does not change the reality that taking a Tesla Cybercab will likely cost a fraction of what a conventional taxi with an internal combustion engine would cost."

Samaha, therefore, is ready to give Musk the benefit of the doubt, preferring to look toward the potential revenue stream the company could enjoy when full self-driving (eventually) comes online.

"As such, investors have been willing to put aside the delays and look at Tesla as a high-risk/high-reward stock. Such stocks have a place in a growth investor's portfolio," sums up Samaha. (To watch Lee Samaha's track record, click here)

Wall Street, for its part, can't come to an agreement on Tesla's risk/reward calculus. With 10 Buys, 8 Holds, and 7 Sells, TSLA carries a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Its 12-month average price target of $398.38 would translate into losses in the low double digits. (See TSLA stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: The Week That Was, The Week Ahead: Macro and Markets, Jan. 25
data.description:
U.S. stocks finished the week mixed as gains in technology shares helped offset weakness in financial stocks and rising rate pressure. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) fell 0.58% to $49,098.71, the S&P 500 (SPX) ended nearly flat, up 0.03% to $6,915.61, and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) outperformed, rising 0.34% to $25,605.47.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meanwhile, interest rates continued to move higher. The 10-year Treasury yield climbed to about 4.23%, reflecting steady inflation concerns and caution ahead of earnings. In commodities, oil (CL) gained 2.78% to $61.24 per barrel as energy demand expectations firmed, while gold (XAUUSD) rose 0.7% to $4,982.50 as investors balanced risk exposure. Bitcoin (BTC) slipped 0.61% to $89,551 as digital assets paused after recent gains.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: CoreWeave (CRWV) Stock Faces Pressure as Lawsuit Exposes AI Build Delays
data.description:
CoreWeave, Inc. (CRWV) is facing a securities fraud lawsuit after a series of sharp stock declines tied to project delays and reduced guidance. The case was filed in the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey and names the company along with several senior executives. The lawsuit claims investors were misled about how quickly CoreWeave could scale its AI data center operations, even as demand for AI computing remained strong.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the center of the case are statements in which CoreWeave claimed it could deploy AI infrastructure at a massive scale and rapidly expand operations. However, according to the complaint, several key data center projects were already facing construction delays that were not fully disclosed. Those delays were linked mainly to Core Scientific, a major infrastructure partner that builds and operates sites used by CoreWeave.

In the meantime, CRWV shares rose 1.30% on Friday, closing at $92.98.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Airbus SE (EADSY) CEO Says Trade Tensions Caused 'Significant' Damage
data.description:
Airbus SE (EADSY) is telling staff that global trade friction is no longer a short-term issue, but a lasting risk that affects daily operations. In an internal memo seen by Reuters, Chief Executive Officer Guillaume Faury said trade disputes caused "significant collateral damage, logistically and financially," during 2025.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to Faury, rising tensions between the U.S. and China have made the industrial environment harder to manage. He added that the start of 2026 is shaped by an unusual number of global crises. As a result, Airbus must rely more on internal strength and tighter planning.

Although these pressures weighed on the business, Faury said Airbus still delivered "good results" overall last year. The company is scheduled to report full earnings on February 19.

On Friday, EADSY shares rose 1.30% to close at $61.38.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Archer Aviation (ACHR) Clears Share Registration as Launch Plans Edge Closer
data.description:
Archer Aviation (ACHR) filed new SEC paperwork that allows a small group of shareholders to resell existing stock. However, the company made clear that it will not receive any cash from these sales. Specifically, the filing registers up to 341,984 shares that were issued earlier as part of a business acquisition. According to the prospectus, "we will not receive any proceeds from the sale of shares by the selling stockholders." As a result, this filing does not represent a capital raise or a new share issuance by Archer.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Instead, the filing mainly gives those shareholders the option to sell shares in the open market over time. Importantly, the company noted that the sellers may choose to sell "some, all, or none" of the shares, meaning no immediate selling pressure is implied. In a broader context, the share count is small relative to Archer's total outstanding stock, which stood at more than 651 million shares as of September 30, 2025.

Meanwhile, ACHR shares dropped 4.44% on Friday, closing at $8.61.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Vanguard Group's $1 Trillion Global Push Signals More Fee Pressure
data.description:
Vanguard Group Inc. is pushing harder outside the U.S. as its non-U.S. assets pass $1 trillion. The firm now manages more than $12 trillion in total assets around the world, and its chief executive, Salim Ramji, said the next phase of growth will come from global markets. He told the Financial Times that there are "incredible opportunities" as more people move cash into investing. At the same time, Vanguard plans to grow its non-U.S. client base from 17 million to nearly 40 million within five years, reflecting a view that many overseas savers still avoid markets.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In regions like the UK and Europe, household savings remain heavily cash-based. According to Ramji, investing in these markets often feels too costly and difficult for everyday savers. As a result, large pools of capital remain outside long-term investments. Meanwhile, several governments are now encouraging people to invest more, both to improve retirement outcomes and to support domestic growth. Vanguard is one of several firms backing a UK effort aimed at shifting savings out of cash.

Fees and Strategic Simplicity

Vanguard is best known for low-cost funds and simple products designed for long-term investors. Recently, the firm cut fees on its £52 billion LifeStrategy fund range and reduced its exposure to UK assets in favor of global stocks. The company said the changes were made in response to client demand rather than market timing. This move fits Vanguard's broader effort to simplify investing while keeping costs low.

Chris McIsaac, who leads Vanguard's non-U.S. business, said assets outside the U.S. have doubled over the past three years. "At this pace, it will take us another five (years) to attract the next $1 trillion," he said. He also noted that index funds and ETFs remain underused in many international portfolios. Meanwhile, Vanguard's pricing gap continues to stand out, with Ramji noting that the average Vanguard fee in Europe is about 14 basis points, compared with an industry average closer to 65 basis points.

This cost advantage places pressure on rivals like BlackRock (BLK), which also benefits from scale. However, Vanguard's ownership model sets it apart, since the firm is owned by its fund investors rather than outside shareholders. As a result, profits are often returned through lower fees.

Using TipRanks' Comparison Tool, we've lined up notable industry rivals to Vanguard Group Inc. in the asset management space. It's a great tool for gaining an in-depth view of each stock and the broader sector.
data.author: Ran Melamed
